summary: Iteration 4 testing of the multi-day campaign feature. Created /app/backend/tests/test_campaign.py covering the new POST /api/sim/{id}/next_day endpoint and all related state transitions (28-day sliding block window, fatigue carryover, long-haul night-stop return auto-rostering, campaign_kpis aggregation, day_start advance, phase guard). All 21 backend pytest tests pass (8 new campaign + 10 legacy occ_backend + 3 pairings). Frontend E2E walked: boot -> DAY 1 roster -> assign CP to long-haul A350 -> start_day -> tick x4 -> end_day -> DEBRIEF shows campaign-kpis panel -> click next-day-btn -> roster reloads with 'DAY 2' header -> prerostered-EGW200 tag 'PRE-ROSTERED · NIGHT-STOP RETURN' visible -> 2nd day cycle increments campaign-kpis to '1 DAY COMPLETED' with day-by-day table populated for D1. All required deliverables verified.

issues: Minor UX inconsistency (not a bug per spec): on the END-OF-DAY-N debrief screen, campaign-kpis still shows 'N-1 DAYS COMPLETED' because days_completed is only incremented inside advance_to_next_day (when player clicks NEXT DAY). E.g. day-2 debrief shows '1 DAY COMPLETED'. Players might expect the day being debriefed to already be counted. Consider either renaming label to 'PRIOR DAYS COMPLETED' or incrementing days_completed in end_day instead of next_day.

action_items: Optional UX: clarify campaign-kpis 'DAYS COMPLETED' label OR increment days_completed inside end_day so the current debriefed day is already counted on the debrief screen.
Optional: literal 'OUT-BACK' string in RosterBoard PAIRING column (carried over from iteration 3 - still not addressed).
critical_code_review_comments: simulation.advance_to_next_day correctly orders operations: capture today's KPIs into campaign_kpis BEFORE resetting state; block_history sliding window correctly capped at 28; fatigue update polarity (operating crew +8..18, non-operating -6..12 with floor 5) matches the spec.
_generate_next_day_flights properly groups long-haul returns by aircraft_reg so multi-crew sets travel together; flight.note set to 'RETURN FROM NIGHT-STOP · crew pre-rostered'.
server.next_day endpoint correctly enforces phase=DEBRIEF (HTTP 400 otherwise) and returns {day_number, pre_rostered_returns, campaign_kpis, state}.
Debrief.jsx campaign-kpis panel safely handles missing campaign_kpis with default {days_completed: 0, per_day: []} fallback. The next-day-btn correctly disables during nextDayBusy.
RosterBoard prerostered-{callsign} testid only renders when flight.note contains 'RETURN FROM NIGHT-STOP' - exact match with backend wording, so contract is tight.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates a fresh game via POST /api/sim/new; test_campaign cycles through 2 days.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All multi-day campaign features green. Backend campaign tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_campaign.py. To re-run: pytest backend/tests/ -v. The single non-blocking UX nit (days_completed lag by one on debrief screen) is documented under design_issues. Long-haul night-stop selection in tests uses lowest block_min LH route to stay under FDP threshold (LHR-JFK ~430min is safest).
rca of the issue: N/A - no defects. All five iteration-4 deliverables verified: (1) next_day endpoint guards phase=DEBRIEF and returns the documented payload; (2) long-haul outbound on day 1 produces a reverse-route flight on day 2 carrying the same crew_id with 'RETURN FROM NIGHT-STOP' note; (3) campaign_kpis.days_completed increments and per_day grows monotonically; (4) crew[].block_history is appended once per next_day call and block_28d_hr equals its sum; (5) operating crew fatigue increases, non-operating crew fatigue decreases. Frontend Debrief surfaces campaign-kpis panel, next-day-btn, and DAY-N header bumps correctly; RosterBoard tags prerostered-{callsign} for the day-2 return rows.
